text: The overhead lights cast a cold, clinical brightness across the wood and glass, their hum blending with the distant whir of machines. I feel small amidst the pipes running overhead—reminded of the endless channels our thoughts and questions travel. The disorderly assembly of parts and boxes on the table speaks to the creative chaos of invention. Here, curiosity pulses: a space filled with potential, projects in gestation. It is both intimidating and invigorating—the promise of becoming lost in making or finding something new.
